categories.name: Literature
categories.description: Understand scientific literature, documents, evidence, and artifacts.

categories.name: Analysis
categories.description: Analyze scientific evidence into reusable schemas, fields, records, and domain-grounded entities.

categories.name: Validation
categories.description: Validate extracted records, resolve conflicts, and evaluate against ground truth.

categories.name: Execution
categories.description: Improve reusable skills from trajectories and feedback.

skills.name: Scientific Document Intake
skills.summary: Map a scientific document package into main text, supplementary files, table-like artifacts, unreadable files, and recommended next actions.

skills.name: Scientific Paper Structure Parsing
skills.summary: Parse main text into normalized paper sections and supplementary references.

skills.name: Task-Relevant Section Localization
skills.summary: Rank paper sections by task relevance using task goal, schema, section type, and supplementary references.

skills.name: Evidence-Aware Claim Classification
skills.summary: Classify scientific text spans as background, method, prediction, experimental observation, measured result, author conclusion, speculation, or unclear.

skills.name: Evidence Source Attribution
skills.summary: Attach traceable evidence source metadata to claims, fields, or records.

skills.name: Supplementary Artifact Discovery
skills.summary: Discover and prioritize supplementary artifacts likely to contain task-relevant evidence or structured data.

skills.name: Multi-format Artifact Reading
skills.summary: Read scientific artifacts into normalized provenance-preserving artifact views.

skills.name: Scientific Table Structure Understanding
skills.summary: Understand table layout, headers, data region, annotation rows, column profiles, and structural warnings.

skills.name: Extraction Schema Interpretation
skills.summary: Interpret task-specific extraction schema into field definitions, required/nullable fields, evidence requirements, validation requirements, and extraction constraints.

skills.name: Schema-Guided Field Mapping
skills.summary: Map source fields, table columns, text spans, or artifact metadata to schema fields.

skills.name: Structured Record Construction
skills.summary: Construct candidate structured records from schema interpretation, field mapping, source values, and provenance.

skills.name: Domain Ontology Alignment
skills.summary: Align extracted mentions or record fields to task-provided ontology concepts, entity types, or controlled vocabulary.

skills.name: Domain Entity Normalization
skills.summary: Normalize aligned domain entities using task-provided domain normalization policy.

skills.name: Domain Entity Validation
skills.summary: Validate normalized entities against task-provided domain validation policy.

skills.name: Domain Negative Pattern Filtering
skills.summary: Filter false positives using task-provided negative pattern policy.

skills.name: Extraction Result Validation
skills.summary: Validate candidate records against schema, evidence requirements, domain validation results, and negative filtering results.

skills.name: Record Deduplication and Conflict Resolution
skills.summary: Deduplicate records and resolve conflicting fields while preserving evidence.

skills.name: Ground Truth Based Evaluation
skills.summary: Compare predicted records with ground truth using task-provided evaluation schema and matching policy.

skills.name: Trajectory Pattern Mining
skills.summary: Mine reusable patterns, failure-repair patterns, tool-use patterns, and candidate skill suggestions from subagent trajectories and tool traces.

skills.name: Human Feedback Integration
skills.summary: Normalize human feedback into corrections, constraints, review signals, and possible skill update signals.

metadata.description: Reusable scientific information extraction seed skill graph. Domain packages provide schemas, ontology, and policies for specific tasks.
metadata.stable_skill_policy: Stable skills are reusable across scientific tasks and must not encode narrow domain-specific extraction skill IDs.
